what_it_does: Defines a React modal for previewing exportable code snippets derived from generated icons, palette edits, extra colors, and pattern token edits, with per-block copy and download actions.
observations: Uses React globals with aliased hooks from the global React object.
Builds JavaScript and CSS patch text with useMemo based on incoming edit props.
Returns null when the modal is not open.
Groups pattern edits into spacing, radii, shadows, motion, and other buckets by token-name prefixes.
Groups extra colors by their group field before emitting CSS variables.
Renders an empty-state message when there are no icon, color, or pattern patches.
Exports the component by assigning it to window.ExportPatch.
standouts: The file is centered on generating human-applied patch snippets rather than directly mutating source files.
Patch output preserves target-file context through titles, file labels, and header comments.
The modal combines browser-state export explanation, preview, clipboard copy, and downloadable patch files in one local UI.
conventions: Uses inline style objects for layout and modal styling.
Uses existing CSS custom properties for colors, typography, radius, and shadows.
Uses design-system button class names for action buttons inside ExportBlock.
Uses early null return for closed modal state.
Uses global window assignment instead of module export syntax.
Uses string templates to assemble source-code snippets.
concerns: Generated icon JavaScript interpolates icon names and bodies into string output without visible escaping for quote, backtick, or template-placeholder characters.
Clipboard writes are not awaited and do not visibly handle failure.
CSS patch generation does not visibly validate color values, roles, group names, or pattern values before inserting them into comments or declarations.
The modal lacks visible ARIA role, focus management, or keyboard handling in this file.
notes: This file is a browser-side patch-export modal that turns in-memory studio edits into copyable or downloadable JS/CSS snippets.
questions: Are generated icon bodies guaranteed not to contain backticks or template interpolation syntax?
Are token values validated before they reach this component?
Should export blocks produce separate filenames when multiple blocks target the same extension?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it shows changed design-system artifacts as patch snippets and lets the user copy or download them, while also displaying an empty state when there is nothing to export.
apparent_themes: Manual handoff from browser state to source files
intention_signals: It appears intended to bridge temporary studio edits and persistent design-system files by making the necessary code fragments visible, labeled, and easy to transfer.
novelty: The component emits source-file patch text directly from UI state, including grouped CSS token sections and a JavaScript object merge for generated icons.
contribution: Contributes a self-contained export surface for translating design-studio edits into human-readable JS and CSS patch snippets, including empty-state handling, grouped token formatting, clipboard transfer, and local file download behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A React global component file defining an export modal that generates, previews, copies, and downloads JS/CSS patch snippets for icons, color tokens, and pattern tokens.
intention_for_embedding: It was for moving browser-held design-system studio edits into persistent source files through labeled manual patch snippets.

symbols.does: Renders the export modal and computes optional icon, color, and pattern patch snippets from props.
symbols.description: ExportPatch is the main modal component. It uses memoized string generation to build JavaScript for generated icons, CSS for palette changes, and CSS for pattern-token edits, then renders an empty state or one ExportBlock per generated patch.

symbols.does: Renders one patch preview with target-file labeling, copy control, download control, and a preformatted code block.
symbols.description: ExportBlock owns the copied indicator state for a single generated patch. It presents the patch title and target file, exposes copy and download actions, and displays the full patch text in a scrollable pre element.

symbols.does: Writes the current patch code to the clipboard and temporarily toggles the copied label.
symbols.description: copy calls navigator.clipboard.writeText when available, sets local copied state to true, and schedules it back to false after 1500 milliseconds. It does not visibly await the clipboard operation or branch on success versus failure.

symbols.does: Creates a text Blob for the patch code and triggers a browser download with a studio-patch extension.
symbols.description: download derives the extension from the target file name, creates an object URL for the patch text, clicks a temporary anchor, and revokes the URL. It maps JavaScript targets to studio-patch.js and all other targets to studio-patch.css.

symbols.does: Normalizes a string into a lowercase CSS-token-friendly slug.
symbols.description: slug converts the input to a string, lowercases it, replaces runs of characters outside lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens with hyphens, then trims leading or trailing hyphens. It is used for CSS variable names derived from color names.
